item.title: Auto CPAP Therapy Guide for Better Sleep and Safer Breathing
item.description: Learn what Auto CPAP therapy is, who benefits from it, and how the device supports healthier breathing throughout the night.
item.content:encoded:
Auto CPAP therapy is one of the most effective ways to manage obstructive sleep apnea and restore consistent nighttime breathing. When treatment is matched correctly to the patient, it helps reduce airway collapse, improves sleep quality, and lowers the health burden created by repeated oxygen drops during the night.

What Is Sleep Apnea?

Sleep apnea is a condition in which breathing repeatedly slows down or stops during sleep. The most common form is obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), where the upper airway narrows or collapses for short periods.

Common signs include:

  • Loud snoring
  • Choking or gasping during sleep
  • Waking with a dry mouth or headache
  • Daytime sleepiness and poor concentration
  • Restless or fragmented sleep

When left untreated, sleep apnea may increase the risk of hypertension, cardiovascular disease, stroke, metabolic imbalance, and reduced daytime performance.

What Is an Auto CPAP Machine?

An Auto CPAP machine is a positive airway pressure device designed to keep the airway open while you sleep. Unlike a fixed-pressure CPAP, an Auto CPAP adjusts pressure automatically within a prescribed range based on your breathing pattern through the night.

This makes it especially useful for patients whose pressure needs vary because of sleep position, sleep stage, nasal congestion, or weight changes.

How Auto CPAP Therapy Works

The system draws room air through a filter, pressurizes it, and sends it through tubing to a mask worn over the nose or nose and mouth. That gentle air pressure acts like a pneumatic splint, preventing the airway from collapsing.

The treatment process typically includes:

  1. Filtering ambient air before delivery.
  2. Detecting breathing resistance and flow limitation.
  3. Increasing or lowering pressure within the clinical range as needed.
  4. Maintaining a more stable oxygen level and more continuous sleep.

Core Components of the System

Every Auto CPAP setup relies on a few essential parts working together:

  • Mask: creates the seal needed to deliver therapy.
  • Headgear: keeps the mask stable without excessive pressure on the skin.
  • Tubing: carries airflow from the device to the mask.
  • Motor unit: generates and regulates therapeutic pressure.
  • Filter: helps keep dust and airborne particles out of the airflow pathway.

Why Patients Benefit From Auto CPAP

When the therapy is used consistently and the mask fits well, patients often notice improvement in a short time.

Short-term benefits:

  • Better sleep continuity
  • Reduced snoring
  • Less morning fatigue
  • Improved alertness and mood

Long-term benefits:

  • Better support for cardiovascular health
  • Lower strain from repeated oxygen desaturation
  • Better adherence when pressure changes are better tolerated
  • More stable daily energy and cognitive function

Who Should Consider It?

Auto CPAP is commonly recommended for patients who:

  • Have diagnosed obstructive sleep apnea
  • Need pressure support that changes through the night
  • Are starting therapy and still optimizing the right pressure range
  • Need a more adaptable home therapy option under medical supervision

Practical Advice for Better Results

To get the best outcome from therapy:

  • Use the device every night, including naps when possible.
  • Check mask fit regularly to reduce leaks.
  • Replace worn accessories before they affect treatment quality.
  • Follow your clinician's pressure settings and follow-up schedule.
  • Clean the system routinely to maintain hygiene and airflow performance.

Final Takeaway

Auto CPAP therapy is more than a sleep device. It is a structured, clinically proven support system for breathing, recovery, and long-term health. With the right mask, proper pressure settings, and consistent use, it can make sleep safer, deeper, and far more restorative.

item.title: CPAP Mask, Cleaning, and Replacement Guide for Daily Use
item.description: A practical guide to choosing the right CPAP mask, cleaning the system correctly, and replacing parts on time for reliable therapy.
item.content:encoded:
Successful CPAP therapy depends on more than pressure settings alone. Mask selection, daily cleaning, and timely replacement of consumable parts all affect comfort, hygiene, and treatment effectiveness. A well-maintained system is easier to tolerate and more likely to deliver consistent long-term results.

Choosing the Right CPAP Mask

The mask is the part patients interact with most, so comfort and fit matter. The main mask styles include:

  • Nasal mask: covers the nose and suits many regular CPAP users.
  • Nasal pillow mask: seals at the nostrils and offers a lighter, more open feel.
  • Full face mask: covers the nose and mouth and is often preferred by mouth breathers or patients with frequent nasal blockage.

The best choice depends on facial structure, sleep position, breathing habits, and how much contact the patient can comfortably tolerate.

Why Routine Cleaning Matters

CPAP equipment is exposed to moisture, skin oils, dust, and daily handling. Without regular cleaning, the system can become less comfortable, less hygienic, and more prone to odor, residue, and reduced seal quality.

Routine cleaning helps:

  • Preserve a reliable mask seal
  • Reduce buildup inside the tubing and humidifier chamber
  • Improve the overall user experience
  • Support safer long-term use of the device

Daily and Weekly Cleaning Steps

Follow the manufacturer instructions for your specific model, but the general routine below works well for most systems.

Exterior Surface

  • Disconnect the device from power before cleaning.
  • Wipe the outer surface with a soft, slightly damp cloth.
  • Avoid harsh chemicals or abrasive materials.

Water Chamber

  • Empty old water every morning.
  • Rinse the chamber and allow it to dry completely.
  • Refill with clean water before the next use, based on the device guidance.

Tubing and Mask Components

  • Wash the tubing and removable mask parts with mild soap and lukewarm water.
  • Rinse thoroughly to remove any residue.
  • Let all parts air-dry away from direct heat or sunlight.

Air Outlet and Connectors

  • Inspect the air outlet and connection points for residue or obstruction.
  • Make sure all parts are dry before reconnecting the system.

Inspection Checklist

Check the system regularly for early signs of wear:

  • Cracks or discoloration in the tubing
  • Stretched headgear or reduced mask stability
  • Cushion wear that causes leaks or facial marks
  • Filter discoloration or visible dust buildup
  • Water chamber cloudiness or mineral residue

Small signs of wear often lead to bigger comfort and performance issues if ignored.

Recommended Replacement Timing

Replacement timing can vary by manufacturer and patient use, but this schedule is a practical baseline:

Part Typical replacement interval
Filter Every 1 month
Mask cushion or full mask About every 6 months
Tubing About every 6 months
Headgear Based on loss of support or fit

If a part becomes damaged, discolored, or difficult to clean before the expected date, it should be replaced sooner.

Tips for Better Daily Adherence

  • Keep a simple cleaning routine at the same time each day.
  • Store the system in a clean, dry place.
  • Keep spare filters and essential accessories available.
  • Review fit issues early instead of tolerating ongoing leaks.

Final Takeaway

Good CPAP care protects both the device and the therapy outcome. When the mask fits well, the system stays clean, and worn parts are replaced on time, treatment becomes more comfortable, more reliable, and easier to maintain every night.

item.title: At-Home Sleep Study
item.description: Professional at-home sleep apnea testing with advanced monitoring devices. Detect obstructive sleep apnea, track oxygen levels, and receive a detailed clinical report — all from the comfort of your home.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 85 OMR

Why Might You Need a Sleep Study?

If you experience any of the following symptoms, you may be at risk for Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) — a condition that can affect your cardiovascular health, breathing stability, and overall quality of life:

  • Loud snoring
  • Morning headaches
  • Excessive daytime sleepiness
  • Observed pauses in breathing during sleep

What Is an At-Home Sleep Study?

An at-home sleep study — also known as an out-of-center sleep test — is performed in the comfort of your own home. A compact, advanced monitoring device records key physiological parameters while you sleep.

The test is specifically designed to detect:

  • Repeated breathing pauses
  • Drops in blood oxygen levels
  • Abnormal nighttime breathing patterns

What Does an At-Home Sleep Study Measure?

Our advanced devices record a comprehensive set of physiological parameters, including:

1. Respiratory Parameters

  • Respiratory effort (chest belt)
  • Nasal airflow
  • Snoring detection

2. Oxygenation

  • Oxygen saturation (SpO₂)
  • Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI)
  • Duration and severity of desaturation events

3. Cardiac Parameters

  • Heart rate
  • Pulse variability

4. Body Position

  • Sleep duration by position
  • Event distribution by position
  • Positional dependency analysis

5. Comprehensive Respiratory Event Analysis

  • AHI (Apnea-Hypopnea Index)
  • RDI (Respiratory Disturbance Index)
  • AI (Apnea Index)
  • HI (Hypopnea Index)
  • OSA / CSA / MSA event counts
  • RERA events
  • Total event duration and frequency
  • Ventilatory stability assessment

How Are the Results Interpreted?

After the test is completed, all data are reviewed and interpreted by a qualified sleep specialist. The final clinical report includes:

  • Severity classification — Normal, Mild, Moderate, or Severe
  • Respiratory phenotype — Obstructive, Central, Mixed, or Positional
  • Oxygenation stability assessment
  • Cardiovascular response analysis
  • Clinical risk evaluation
  • Evidence-based treatment recommendations

A complete report is typically issued within 48 business hours.

How the Service Works

If your physician prescribes a home sleep study, the process is as follows:

  1. Obtain a prescription from your doctor.
  2. Contact us to schedule an appointment.
  3. Device installation — Our trained technician visits your home to install the device and provide instructions.
  4. Sleep as you normally would — At least 6 hours of recording is recommended.
  5. Return the device the following day.
  6. Receive your report and review the results with your physician.

Why Choose Our At-Home Sleep Study Service?

  • No need to visit a hospital or sleep lab
  • Maximum comfort in your own home
  • Flexible scheduling
  • Lower cost compared to in-lab testing
  • Fast and accurate results

Important Instructions Before Your Sleep Test

During the Day

  • Avoid daytime naps
  • Avoid coffee, strong tea, energy drinks, and smoking after the afternoon
  • Engage only in light activity; avoid intense exercise

Before Bed

  • Eat a light dinner 2–3 hours before bedtime
  • Take a warm shower to relax your body
  • Wear comfortable cotton clothing

At Bedtime

  • Keep your room dark and quiet
  • Maintain a comfortable room temperature
  • Avoid phone and television use before sleep
  • Practice gentle deep breathing and relaxation
  • Do not worry about the device or wires — you will quickly adjust

Additional Notes

  • Go to bed at your usual time
  • If you wake during the night, relax and try to return to sleep
  • A minimum of 6 hours of sleep is necessary for accurate results

item.title: Ozone CPAP Cleaner and Sanitizer
item.description: A portable ozone-based sanitizer designed to disinfect CPAP and BiPAP accessories with multiple cleaning modes and rechargeable operation.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 25 OMR

This compact cleaner uses ozone sterilization to help disinfect respiratory accessories and selected daily-use items. Its portable form factor and rechargeable battery make it practical for home routines and travel kits.

Key Highlights

  • Ozone-based cleaning process designed for masks, tubing, and related accessories.
  • Multiple disinfection modes to match different cleaning needs.
  • Rechargeable, compact, and easy to carry for home or travel use.

Ideal For

  • Users who want a portable sanitizing option for PAP accessories.
  • Travel kits and compact home setups.
  • People maintaining masks, tubing, humidifier parts, and similar small items.

Gallery

item.title: Aeonmed Auto CPAP AS100A
item.description: A modern automatic CPAP system with pressure response, exhalation comfort, humidification, and mask-fit support for home therapy.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 200 OMR

The Aeonmed AS100A is designed to deliver dependable CPAP therapy with a patient-friendly interface and comfort-focused features. It supports pressure automation, humidity control, and easier acclimatization at bedtime.

Key Highlights

  • Automatic pressure adjustment based on overnight breathing behavior.
  • Pressure relief and auto-ramp features to improve comfort at sleep onset.
  • Integrated humidifier and mask-fit support for a smoother therapy setup.

Ideal For

  • Users starting home therapy for obstructive sleep apnea.
  • Patients who want an auto-adjusting CPAP platform.
  • Setups that need humidification and comfort-focused features in one device.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: CPAP Chin Strap
item.description: A simple mouth-closure support accessory that encourages nasal breathing and helps reduce dry mouth and therapy air leaks.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 8 OMR

This chin strap is designed to support a closed-mouth sleeping posture and improve comfort during PAP therapy. It is especially useful for nasal and pillow mask users who experience mouth opening during sleep.

Key Highlights

  • Encourages nasal breathing and helps reduce dry mouth overnight.
  • Can reduce mouth leaks that affect CPAP therapy effectiveness.
  • Soft, adjustable design for everyday home use.

Ideal For

  • People who sleep with an open mouth.
  • Nasal or pillow mask users who want better seal performance.
  • Users looking for a lightweight accessory to improve comfort.

Gallery

item.title: CPAP Hose Cover
item.description: A soft fleece tubing wrap that improves comfort, helps reduce condensation, and limits hose noise during PAP therapy.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 5 OMR

This fleece hose cover adds an insulating layer around standard PAP tubing to make therapy quieter and more comfortable. It also helps reduce the rainout effect caused by temperature differences inside the hose.

Key Highlights

  • Helps reduce condensation inside the tubing.
  • Adds a softer surface where the hose touches the skin or bedding.
  • Can reduce disturbance from hose movement and noise.

Ideal For

  • Users with humidified PAP therapy.
  • People sensitive to cold tubing against the skin.
  • Sleep setups that need better hose comfort and control.

Gallery

item.title: CPAP Masks
item.description: A curated CPAP mask offering that covers full-face, nasal, and nasal pillow options to match different therapy and comfort needs.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 30 OMR

Choosing the right mask is one of the most important parts of successful PAP therapy. This product category helps patients find a fit that balances seal quality, comfort, and freedom of movement.

Key Highlights

  • Includes options for full-face, nasal, and nasal pillow therapy styles.
  • Designed to reduce leakage and improve stability during sleep.
  • Supports better comfort and adherence by matching the mask to the user’s breathing pattern.

Ideal For

  • Users selecting a first CPAP mask.
  • Patients who want to change mask style for better comfort.
  • Home therapy setups that need the right balance of seal and movement.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: CPAP Silicone Protector Mat
item.description: A non-slip silicone mat that protects bedside surfaces, reduces vibration, and helps manage moisture under PAP devices.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 6.9 OMR

This silicone protector mat creates a stable base for CPAP and BiPAP devices on bedside tables and clinical surfaces. It helps absorb vibration, reduce device movement, and protect furniture from condensation or spills.

Key Highlights

  • Non-slip surface that helps keep the device secure.
  • Supports noise and vibration reduction during operation.
  • Water-resistant design helps protect furniture from moisture.

Ideal For

  • Bedside PAP setups with humidifiers.
  • Users who want a cleaner and quieter device area.
  • Clinics or homes that want added surface protection.

Gallery

item.title: Heyer CPAP Tubing 19 mm
item.description: A standard 19 mm CPAP hose with universal connectors for stable airflow and broad compatibility with PAP devices and masks.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 10 OMR

This standard-size tubing is designed to provide consistent airflow between the device and the mask. Its flexible construction supports daily use while maintaining compatibility with most standard PAP setups.

Key Highlights

  • Standard 19 mm tubing format with universal 22 mm connectors.
  • Designed for stable airflow and reduced resistance.
  • Flexible construction that supports movement during sleep.

Ideal For

  • Routine CPAP or BiPAP hose replacement.
  • Users who need broad compatibility with standard masks.
  • Home therapy setups needing a dependable everyday hose.

Gallery

item.title: Heyer Auto CPAP Device 20A Pro
item.description: An advanced automatic CPAP device with intelligent pressure adjustment, comfort relief, and humidification for dependable nightly therapy.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 285 OMR

The Heyer 20A Pro is designed for patients who need stable, comfortable airway support at home. It combines automatic pressure response, ramp support, and humidification to improve adherence and reduce treatment discomfort.

Key Highlights

  • Automatic pressure adjustment that responds to breathing patterns throughout the night.
  • AS-ELEX pressure relief and auto-ramp support for a more comfortable sleep transition.
  • Integrated humidification and heated tubing support to reduce dryness and improve comfort.

Ideal For

  • Patients prescribed CPAP or APAP therapy for obstructive sleep apnea.
  • Users who want a more comfortable setup with ramp and exhalation relief.
  • Home therapy programs that need dependable nightly use.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: Heyer BiPAP Device BV30ST Pro
item.description: A high-support BiPAP platform with advanced ventilation modes, pressure control, and monitoring for more complex respiratory therapy needs.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 385 OMR

The Heyer BV30ST Pro is built for patients who need a higher level of ventilatory assistance than standard CPAP therapy. It offers multiple clinical modes and controlled pressure delivery for home and clinic use.

Key Highlights

  • Multiple ventilation modes including CPAP, Auto CPAP, S, Auto S, T, S/T, and VGPS support.
  • Intelligent pressure control for more stable respiratory support.
  • Suitable for more advanced therapy planning in both home and clinical settings.

Ideal For

  • Patients with more complex sleep-disordered breathing needs.
  • Cases requiring spontaneous or timed support modes.
  • Providers looking for broader ventilation flexibility in one device.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: CPAP Tubing Cleaning Brush
item.description: A dual-ended cleaning brush for PAP tubing and accessories that helps remove residue, moisture, and buildup from inside the hose.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 3.9 OMR

This dual-ended brush is designed to clean long tubing and small respiratory components with one tool. Regular use helps maintain airflow quality and supports better equipment hygiene.

Key Highlights

  • Dual-ended format for full-length tubing and smaller components.
  • Helps remove moisture, residue, and debris inside the hose.
  • Flexible construction that supports easy navigation through standard tubing.

Ideal For

  • Routine maintenance of CPAP and BiPAP tubing.
  • Users who wash hoses regularly and want more complete cleaning.
  • Home care kits focused on device hygiene.

Gallery

item.title: Ortino Positioning Wedge
item.description: A positional support wedge system designed to help maintain side-sleep posture and improve comfort for selected sleep-positioning needs.
item.content:encoded:
Ortino is presented as a positional support solution for users who need structured side-sleep support. It can be useful in routines where stable positioning and body support are part of a sleep-comfort plan.

Key Highlights

  • Structured wedge-style support for positional sleep comfort.
  • Suitable for side-sleep routines that need a more stable body position.
  • Includes brochure and sizing guide PDFs for review before selection.

Ideal For

  • Users exploring positional sleep support products.
  • Sleep setups that require better side-position stability.
  • Customers who want to review brochure and sizing guidance before purchase.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF
  • Sizing Guide PDF

item.title: Portable Oxygen Concentrator ZY-1S
item.description: A portable 1-7 L oxygen concentrator with a compact body, large display, and dependable oxygen delivery for daily respiratory support.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 225 OMR

The ZY-1S is built to deliver portable oxygen therapy in a compact, handle-equipped form factor. It is positioned as a practical option for home use and everyday mobility where stable oxygen support and easy operation matter.

Key Highlights

  • 1-7 L flow range with portable carry handle and compact dimensions.
  • Large front display designed for quick reading and easier operation.
  • Suitable for home support routines that value portability and simple controls.

Ideal For

  • Users needing a compact oxygen concentrator for home environments.
  • Respiratory support routines that benefit from portability.
  • Setups that need easier handling and visible front-panel information.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: Positional Snoring Belt
item.description: A non-invasive positional therapy belt designed to discourage back sleeping and support side-sleep posture for snoring control.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 49 OMR

This positional belt is designed for users whose snoring or breathing disturbances worsen when sleeping on the back. Its ergonomic support encourages side sleeping to help improve airflow and sleep quality.

Key Highlights

  • Helps reduce time spent sleeping in the supine position.
  • Supports a more stable side-sleeping posture through the night.
  • Modular design with soft cover and supportive foam construction.

Ideal For

  • People with positional snoring.
  • Users who need help maintaining a side-sleep posture.
  • Home-based positional therapy plans.

Gallery

item.title: SnoreRx Mandibular Advancement Device
item.description: An adjustable oral appliance for snoring control that helps reposition the jaw and support better airflow during sleep.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 49 OMR

This adjustable mandibular advancement device is intended to reduce snoring by gently moving the lower jaw forward. Its oral design aims to improve nighttime airflow without a mask-based setup.

Key Highlights

  • Adjustable oral design focused on snoring control.
  • Supports airway opening by advancing the mandible.
  • Compact format for users seeking a non-mask sleep aid option.

Ideal For

  • Adults looking for a mask-free snoring solution.
  • Users who prefer a compact oral appliance approach.
  • People exploring clinician-guided oral sleep therapy.

Gallery

item.title: Waterpulse Nasal Wash Bottle YT-300
item.description: A 300 ml manual nasal rinse bottle designed for simple home sinus care, congestion relief, and everyday nasal hygiene.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 6.9 OMR

The Waterpulse YT-300 is a practical rinse bottle for regular nasal cleansing at home. It supports saline flow through the nasal passages to help remove mucus, dust, and allergens and improve breathing comfort.

Key Highlights

  • 300 ml manual rinse format for simple daily use.
  • Supports relief from congestion, allergies, sinus pressure, and dry nasal passages.
  • Compact, easy-to-handle design suitable for household use.

Ideal For

  • Users wanting a straightforward manual nasal rinse option.
  • People managing daily congestion or seasonal allergy symptoms.
  • Families seeking a basic home sinus-care product.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery

item.title: ZEUS Hypoglossal Nerve Stimulator
item.description: A non-implantable external stimulation device designed to reduce snoring by supporting tongue muscle tone and airway openness during sleep.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 185 OMR

ZEUS is a discreet external device that applies gentle stimulation beneath the chin to support upper-airway tone during sleep. It is designed for users seeking a non-invasive option for snoring management with a research-backed concept.

Key Highlights

  • External non-implantable stimulation approach for nighttime snoring management.
  • Designed to support tongue muscle tone and help maintain airflow.
  • Includes reference papers that support deeper clinical review.

Ideal For

  • Adults looking for a non-mask, non-implant snoring solution.
  • Users interested in an external stimulation-based sleep aid.
  • Clinician-guided snoring therapy pathways.

Downloads

  • Product PDF
  • Product PDF
  • Product PDF
  • Thorax 2016 Pengo thorax journal article

Gallery

item.title: Oniris Mandibular Advancement Device
item.description: A non-invasive oral appliance that gently advances the lower jaw to support airway openness and reduce snoring and mild to moderate OSA symptoms.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 69 OMR

Oniris is a clinically recognized mandibular advancement device designed to reduce upper-airway collapse during sleep. By moving the lower jaw slightly forward, it helps maintain airflow and supports quieter, more stable sleep.

Key Highlights

  • Non-invasive oral therapy option for snoring and selected OSA cases.
  • Supports airway patency by advancing the lower jaw during sleep.
  • Includes brochure and clinical reference documents for deeper review.

Ideal For

  • Adults with snoring or mild to moderate obstructive sleep apnea.
  • Patients seeking an alternative to conventional mask-based therapy.
  • Clinician-guided treatment pathways that include oral sleep devices.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF
  • Brochure PDF
  • European Respiratory Society 2017 Late Breaking Abstract IRIS
  • International Journal of Dentistry 2024 Lo Giudice Indications for Dental Specialists for Treating Obstructive Sleep
  • Marty Study Journal of Prosthodontics (2015)
  • Product PDF
  • Oniris Iris Study Results at 12 Months
  • Product PDF
  • Pépin JL Heat moulded versus custom made MAD RCT non inferiority THORAX 2019
  • Product PDF

Gallery

item.title: Nasopro Electric Nasal Irrigation System
item.description: An electric nasal irrigation device that combines gentle rinsing and suction support to improve nasal hygiene and breathing comfort.
item.content:encoded:
Price: 49 OMR

The Nasopro electric system is designed to support daily nasal hygiene and more complete cleaning than traditional rinse methods. It can help users manage mucus, allergens, and congestion while improving airflow comfort.

Key Highlights

  • Combines irrigation support with a more advanced cleaning approach.
  • Helps clear mucus, allergens, and environmental irritants from the nasal passages.
  • Designed for users seeking a modern home nasal care routine.

Ideal For

  • People dealing with congestion, sinus discomfort, or allergic irritation.
  • Users who want a powered alternative to manual nasal rinsing.
  • Home respiratory care routines focused on nasal hygiene.

Downloads

  • Brochure PDF

Gallery
